project.name: UK Road Traffic Analytics
project.description: A data warehouse for UK road traffic analytics using a star schema design with dimension and fact tables, ETL pipeline loading 1.1M+ directional traffic flow records, and business intelligence queries for regional traffic analysis, year-over-year growth, and geospatial insights.

nodes.name: create_database.sql
nodes.summary: Creates the UK_Road_Traffic_DW data warehouse database on SQL Server.

nodes.name: create_tables.sql
nodes.summary: Defines the star schema with 4 dimension tables (DimDate, DimRegion, DimLocalAuthority, DimCountPoint) and 1 fact table (FactTrafficFlowDirection) with foreign key relationships. Also includes utility queries for table verification and row counts.

nodes.name: business_queries.sql
nodes.summary: Contains 6 analytical SQL queries: regional traffic summary, top-10 busiest roads, year-over-year growth trend, HGV vs passenger vehicle mix by region, local authority 5-year growth comparison, and road category distribution with geospatial traffic hotspot mapping.

nodes.name: indexes.sql
nodes.summary: Creates non-clustered indexes on the fact table's 4 foreign key columns (DateKey, RegionKey, LocalAuthorityKey, CountPointKey) to optimize join performance for analytical queries.

nodes.name: DimDate
nodes.summary: Date dimension table with calendar attributes: DateKey (PK), FullDate, Year, Quarter, Month, MonthName, Day, DayOfWeek, DayName, IsWeekend, and FiscalYear. Enables time-based slicing of traffic data.

nodes.name: DimRegion
nodes.summary: Region dimension table storing UK geographic regions with RegionKey (identity PK), RegionID, RegionName, and RegionONSCode. Maps to source data region_id/region_name/region_ons_code columns.

nodes.name: DimLocalAuthority
nodes.summary: Local authority dimension with foreign key to DimRegion, storing LocalAuthorityID, Name, and Code. Represents the administrative geography level below region.

nodes.name: DimCountPoint
nodes.summary: Count point dimension storing traffic sensor metadata: CountPointID, RoadName, RoadCategory, RoadType, Latitude/Longitude coordinates, and LinkLengthKm. Each count point is a physical traffic measurement location.

nodes.name: FactTrafficFlowDirection
nodes.summary: Central fact table with 1.1M+ rows recording directional AADF traffic counts per count point per year. Contains 12 vehicle-type measures (PedalCycles through AllMotorVehicles) plus DirectionOfTravel. Links to all 4 dimension tables via foreign keys. LoadDate tracks ETL timestamp.

nodes.name: Regional Traffic Summary
nodes.summary: Aggregates total motor vehicles, HGVs, and all vehicles by region for 2023. Joins FactTrafficFlowDirection with DimRegion and DimDate, grouped by RegionName, ordered by total descending.

nodes.name: Top 10 Busiest Roads
nodes.summary: Identifies the 10 roads with highest total traffic since 2020, filtering for roads with over 1M total motor vehicles. Shows RoadName, RoadCategory, TotalTraffic, and AvgDailyFlow.

nodes.name: Year-over-Year Growth Trend
nodes.summary: Uses CTE with LAG window function to calculate annual traffic growth percentage across all years. Shows TotalTraffic, PrevYear, and YoY_GrowthPct for trend analysis.

nodes.name: HGV vs Passenger Vehicle Mix
nodes.summary: Compares heavy goods vehicle traffic to passenger vehicle traffic by region, calculating HGV percentage of total motor vehicles. Useful for freight corridor identification.

nodes.name: Local Authority 5-Year Growth
nodes.summary: Uses CTE with PIVOT-style CASE expressions to compare 2018 vs 2023 traffic per local authority, calculating 5-year growth percentage. Identifies fastest-growing and declining areas.

nodes.name: Road Category Traffic Distribution
nodes.summary: Aggregates traffic by road category (Motorway, A-road, B-road, etc.) showing count point coverage and average flow per point.

nodes.name: Geospatial Traffic Hotspots
nodes.summary: Returns lat/long coordinates with traffic volumes for 2023 count points exceeding 50K total motor vehicles. Designed for Power BI map visualization, joining all 4 dimensions to the fact table.

edges.label: Database must exist before tables are created
Creates 4 non-clustered indexes on FK columns
All queries read from tables defined here
All 6 queries use the fact table as primary source
3 of 6 queries join to DimDate for year filtering
3 of 6 queries join to DimCountPoint for road/geo data
Indexes reference columns defined in create_tables

layers.name: Database Setup
layers.description: Database initialization — creates the UK_Road_Traffic_DW database instance.

layers.name: Schema Definition
layers.description: Star schema DDL — 4 dimension tables and 1 fact table with foreign key relationships forming the core data model.

layers.name: Performance Optimization
layers.description: Index definitions that optimize query performance on the fact table's foreign key join columns.

layers.name: Business Analytics
layers.description: Analytical queries for traffic reporting — regional summaries, trend analysis, vehicle mix, growth comparisons, and geospatial hotspot mapping.

tour.title: Database Initialization
tour.description: The project starts by creating a dedicated SQL Server data warehouse called UK_Road_Traffic_DW. This is the foundation — all tables, indexes, and queries operate within this database.

tour.title: Star Schema Design
tour.description: The data model follows a classic star schema pattern. Four dimension tables (DimDate, DimRegion, DimLocalAuthority, DimCountPoint) surround a central fact table. Each dimension captures a different analytical axis: time, geography (two levels), and measurement location.

tour.title: Fact Table — The Heart of the Warehouse
tour.description: FactTrafficFlowDirection is the central fact table with 1.1M+ rows. Each row records directional AADF (Annual Average Daily Flow) traffic counts at a specific count point for a given year. It stores 12 vehicle-type measures from pedal cycles to articulated HGVs, linked to all 4 dimensions via foreign keys.

tour.title: Performance Indexes
tour.description: Four non-clustered indexes are created on the fact table's foreign key columns. These dramatically speed up the JOIN operations that every analytical query relies on, since the fact table has over 1 million rows.

tour.title: Regional and Temporal Analysis
tour.description: The business queries begin with broad aggregations — total traffic by region for 2023, and a year-over-year growth trend using window functions (LAG). These answer 'where is traffic heaviest?' and 'how is traffic changing over time?'

tour.title: Deep-Dive Analytics
tour.description: More targeted queries drill into specific dimensions: the top 10 busiest roads, HGV vs passenger vehicle mix by region (freight corridor analysis), local authority 5-year growth comparison (2018 vs 2023), and road category distribution. These use CTEs, HAVING filters, and CASE-based pivots.

tour.title: Geospatial Visualization Layer
tour.description: The final query outputs lat/long coordinates with traffic volumes for high-traffic count points (>50K vehicles in 2023). This is designed to feed Power BI map visualizations, joining all 4 dimensions to provide full geographic context.
